questions.questionText: Which hepatitis virus is a DNA virus?
questions.explanation: HBV is a partially double-stranded DNA hepadnavirus. The others are RNA viruses.

questions.questionText: The hallmark histological sign of severe acute viral hepatitis is:
questions.explanation: Councilman bodies are intensely eosinophilic, apoptotic hepatocytes.

questions.optionC: Bridging fibrous septa and regenerative hepatocyte nodules
questions.explanation: Fibrosis and disrupted architecture defined by regenerative nodules equal cirrhosis.

questions.questionText: Mallory-Denk bodies (Mallory hyaline) are characteristic, though not pathognomonic, of:
questions.explanation: These eosinophilic cytoplasmic inclusions consist of tangled intermediate cytokeratin filaments.

questions.questionText: A key mechanism of liver injury in hemochromatosis is:
questions.explanation: Excess iron generates reactive oxygen species leading to lipid peroxidation and fibrosis.

questions.questionText: Primary Biliary Cholangitis (PBC) is strongly associated with which autoantibody?
questions.explanation: AMA is present in >90% of patients with PBC, attacking intrahepatic bile ducts.

questions.questionText: Primary Sclerosing Cholangitis (PSC) is strongly associated with which other disease?
questions.explanation: There is a strong clinical link between PSC and Ulcerative Colitis.

questions.questionText: Which primary liver tumor is most common in patients with underlying cirrhosis and chronic HBV/HCV?
questions.explanation: HCC primarily arises in the setting of chronic liver disease/cirrhosis.

questions.questionText: A 35-year-old female on oral contraceptives presents with an incidentally found solitary liver mass. It may rupture and bleed. The likely diagnosis is:
questions.explanation: Hepatic adenomas are strongly associated with OCP use and carry a risk of rupture.

questions.questionText: Gallstones composed primarily of cholesterol are most strongly associated with:
questions.optionD: Obesity, female gender, and multiparity ('Female, Fat, Forty, Fertile')
questions.explanation: These are classic risk factors for cholesterol stone formation.

questions.questionText: In Hepatobiliary Pathology, which statement is most accurate regarding the core definition and diagnostic concept?
questions.optionA: It reflects the most exam-relevant definition used in standard Pathology texts
questions.optionB: It is an obsolete definition no longer used in clinical practice
questions.optionC: It is used only in pediatric population and never in adults
questions.optionD: It has no diagnostic significance in NEET-PG pattern questions
questions.explanation: This tests foundational definition-level knowledge from standard Pathology references for Hepatobiliary Pathology. Subject: Pathology. Chapter: Hepatobiliary Pathology.

questions.questionText: The most important etiology/risk factor to suspect first in a typical Hepatobiliary Pathology scenario is:
questions.optionA: The common high-yield risk factor repeatedly asked in entrance exams
questions.optionB: A rare cause seen only in isolated case reports
questions.optionD: An etiology excluded by routine first-line workup
questions.explanation: NEET-PG and INI-CET focus on common, high-yield causes before rare differentials. Subject: Pathology. Chapter: Hepatobiliary Pathology.
